gene_description: Melanocortin 1 Receptor — G-protein coupled receptor on melanocytes controlling pigmentation. Loss-of-function variants (red hair phenotype) alter pain sensitivity and increase anesthetic requirement by ~19% through melanocortin-opioid receptor system crosstalk.

source.title: Anesthetic Requirement is Increased in Redheads
source.finding: Anesthetic requirement in redheads (MC1R variant carriers) was increased by 19% (desflurane MAC 6.2 vs 5.2 vol%), a highly statistically significant difference (p=0.0004), confirming altered pain sensitivity in MC1R loss-of-function carriers.

TT.phenotype: Increased Anesthetic Requirement — Red Hair Phenotype
TT.effect: Homozygous for the R151C variant. Major determinant of red hair phenotype. Approximately 19% higher anesthetic requirement (MAC increase). Enhanced pain sensitivity across multiple pain modalities. Increased responsiveness to certain kappa-opioid agonists (pentazocine, nalbuphine). Mechanism involves melanocortin-opioid receptor system crosstalk in both peripheral and central pathways.
TT.recommendation: Increase volatile anesthetic doses by approximately 20%. Plan for potentially higher analgesic requirements perioperatively. Local anesthetics may also have reduced efficacy — consider higher doses of lidocaine/bupivacaine. However, kappa-opioid agonists may be more effective. Communicate MC1R status to anesthesia team preoperatively.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carrier of R151C. May contribute to red or auburn hair depending on other MC1R variants. Intermediate increase in anesthetic requirement and pain sensitivity. Partial loss of MC1R function.
TC.recommendation: Be aware of potentially increased anesthetic and analgesic needs. Monitor depth of anesthesia carefully. Standard starting doses with readiness to titrate upward.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carrier of R151C. May contribute to red or auburn hair depending on other MC1R variants. Intermediate increase in anesthetic requirement and pain sensitivity. Partial loss of MC1R function.
CT.recommendation: Be aware of potentially increased anesthetic and analgesic needs. Monitor depth of anesthesia carefully. Standard starting doses with readiness to titrate upward.

CC.effect: Wild-type at this position. Normal MC1R function and standard pain sensitivity. Typical anesthetic requirement.
CC.recommendation: Standard anesthetic and analgesic dosing per clinical protocols.

TT.phenotype: Increased Anesthetic Requirement — Red Hair Variant
TT.effect: Homozygous for the R160W variant. Another major red hair determinant. Combined with rs1805007, accounts for approximately 60% of red hair cases. Similar increase in anesthetic requirement and pain sensitivity as R151C.
TT.recommendation: Same precautions as rs1805007 TT. Increase anesthetic doses by ~20%. Plan for higher analgesic needs. Inform anesthesia team.

TC.effect: Heterozygous R160W carrier. Partial effect on pigmentation and pain sensitivity.
TC.recommendation: Monitor anesthetic depth. Be prepared to titrate upward if needed.

CT.effect: Heterozygous R160W carrier. Partial effect on pigmentation and pain sensitivity.
CT.recommendation: Monitor anesthetic depth. Be prepared to titrate upward if needed.

CC.effect: Wild-type at this position. Normal MC1R function.
